_doc: Cheat-sheet study notes for CCNA objective 1.1 — network devices (client, server, switch, router, firewall). Author-original summary, NOT a transcript of any copyrighted source. Schema version: cheat-sheet v1.

sections.title: Client vs Server
sections.tip: Same device can be both — the role depends on who is asking and who is answering in that conversation.
examples: Friend pulling a file off your laptop → you = server

sections.title: Switch (LAN)
bullets: Connects many devices (PCs, printers, IP phones, servers)

sections.title: Router (Internet)
bullets: Sends packets across networks based on destination IP
Fewer ports than a switch — boundary device, not access device

sections.title: Firewall (Security)
bullets: Controls traffic — **ALLOW or DENY** based on rules
Protects networks and hosts from attackers
Two types: **Network firewall** (hardware, guards a whole network) and **Host firewall** (software, guards one machine)

oneLineSummary: Client requests → Server responds → Switch connects locally → Router connects networks → Firewall protects
sourceLabel: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 1 — Network Devices
